title: Pasqal Introduces New Integration with NVIDIA CUDA-Q to Enhance Its Hybrid Quantum Computing Environment for HPC - Pasqal
why:
- Pasqal has integrated the NVIDIA CUDA-Q platform with its Quantum Resource Management Interface (QRMI), enabling CUDA-Q workloads to be scheduled and orchestrated on Pasqal neutral-atom quantum systems through standard Slurm HPC workflows.
- This integration allows quantum processors (QPUs) to act as native accelerators alongside CPUs and GPUs in heterogeneous high-performance computing (HPC) environments.
- QRMI exposes QPUs as schedulable resources within Slurm, allowing secure authentication, allocation, and monitoring of QPUs using existing HPC interfaces without changes to core operating models.
- The deployment will be first available at CINECA, integrating Pasqal’s QPU with the Leonardo pre-exascale supercomputer, enabling Slurm-native hybrid GPU–QPU workloads.
- The integration is already available using QPUs on Pasqal’s cloud platform, facilitating easier quantum adoption for HPC users.
